# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

# Use an older base image that comes with Python 2.7 pre-installed
FROM python:2.7-slim

# Make a directory to work inside in docker container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy requirements file with the python dependencies to the working directory
COPY requirements.txt /app

# Install the dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy current directory (where the Dockerfile is) contents into the container at /app
COPY . /app

# Set the flask application
ENV FLASK_APP=app.py

# Expose port 5000
EXPOSE 5000

# Command to run on container start
CMD [ "python", "./app.py" ]
